name: ESP32 Hosted Co-processor Update
description: ESP32 Hosted update platform to manage co-processor firmware updates.

config_entries.label: Type
config_entries.description: The update mode to use. Must be either embedded or http.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the update entity. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: The icon to use for the update entity in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: How often to check for updates. Defaults to 6h.

config_entries.label: Path
config_entries.description: Path to the co-processor firmware binary file (.bin). The path is relative to your ESPHome configuration file.

config_entries.label: Sha256
config_entries.description: SHA256 hash of the firmware binary file. This is used to verify the integrity of the firmware both at compile time and at runtime before flashing to the co-processor.

config_entries.label: Source
config_entries.description: URL to the JSON manifest file containing available firmware versions.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the update entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Specifying an `id` without a `name` will
